=======================

Perth, the capital of Western Australia, is a vibrant city with plenty of exciting things to do for travelers. From exploring its scenic parks and beaches to indulging in its cultural and culinary scene, Perth has something for everyone. In this video, we’ll highlight some of the top things to do in Perth.

Nature lovers will enjoy the beautiful parks and beaches that Perth has to offer. Kings Park and Botanic Garden is a must-visit destination, offering sweeping views of the city skyline and the Swan River. The park spans over 400 hectares and features walking trails, picnic areas, and a playground for children. Another great park to explore is Yanchep National Park, home to kangaroos, koalas, and a series of caves to explore.

If you’re looking for adventure, head to Rottnest Island, located just off the coast of Perth. This car-free island is home to stunning beaches, hidden coves, and a variety of water sports activities, such as snorkeling and diving. Another great outdoor activity is hiking in the stunning Perth Hills, which offer a variety of scenic trails for all levels of fitness.

For a cultural experience, visit the Art Gallery of Western Australia, which houses a collection of over 17,000 pieces of art, including works by famous Australian artists. The Perth Cultural Centre is also worth a visit, with its array of museums, galleries, and theaters.

If you’re a foodie, Perth has a thriving culinary scene. Visit the Fremantle Markets to sample local produce, and try some of the city’s best coffee at one of the many specialty cafes around town. For a unique culinary experience, head to the Swan Valley wine region, just a short drive from the city, to sample some of Western Australia’s best wines and gourmet food.

Finally, for a bit of history, head to Fremantle, a charming port city located just south of Perth. Visit the Fremantle Prison, which was built in the 1850s and operated until 1991, to learn about the city’s convict past. The Fremantle Fishing Boat Harbour is also worth a visit, with its seafood restaurants and lively atmosphere.

Overall, Perth has something for everyone, from nature lovers and adventure seekers to culture enthusiasts and foodies. This video highlights just a few of the many things to do in this beautiful city, so start planning your Perth adventure today!

videos.title: Perth Top Attractions: Beautiful Places to Visit in Western Australia: Perth Australia Travel Guide
videos.description:
If you are travelling to Perth and are looking for beautiful places to see or things to do then this ultimate travel guide to Western Australia takes you to see some of the top tourist attractions to visit for your travel itinerary. The list of top 10 tourist attractions are in no particular order starting with:

1. Kings Park and Botanic Gardens
Situated just a few minutes away from theCBD - this is a magnificent sanctuary providing vast expands of rolling greens and natural bushland - you can easily spend a day here, have a stroll, picnic, discover the many plants the botanic garden has to offer - the park is absolutely free. 

2. Elizabeth Quay
You can start your day for coffee and breakfast here. If you are a late starter then you can have lunch at The Island - https://www.theislandeq.com.au. For further information on Elizabeth Quay visit https://developmentwa.com.au/projects/redevelopment/elizabeth-quay/see-do/food-drink2. 

3. Matagarup Bridge
 The bridge will provide you with aa dose of adrenalin rush - you can book your zip lining and/ or bridge climbing here. For further information visit the website. https://www.zipclimb.com.au

4. Perth Laneways
London Court - this is by far the most photographed lane way/arcade in Perth - for further information visit https://londoncourt.com.au
Wolf Lane - walk the colourful and vibrant laneway- you will find shops, cafes and bars around here. Shafto Lane - also popular among locals - you will find restaurants and bars here.
Chun Wa Lane located in Northbridge has many asian restaurants available.

5. Fremantle
The historic port city of Fremantle is another attraction that should be on your list of places to see in Perth. Located just 30 minutes away by train from Perth -  is a vibrant city rich in culture and history. It has many cafes, restaurants and the most popular markets for you to discover. 
The Fremantle Markets. Established since 1897 and still running as a market today. You can head down to the market in the morning for breakfast and coffee - it gets really busy so come in early. For further information please check http://www.fremantlemarkets.com.au

6. Rottnest Island
#Rottnestisland   is truly a premier holiday destination in Australia- it is a favourite  getaway for locals and tourists and the perfect weekend trip from #perth  #westernaustralia  The island is home to stunning beaches, abundance of marine life, spectacular sceneries and home to the happiest animal in the world - #Quokkas. 
For further information on Rottnest Island please check the website - https://www.rottnestisland.com/visit/getting-around
How to spend your day in Rottnest Island - please check:
https://destinationlesstravel.com/rottnest-island-day-trip/

7. Margaret River Region
If you are looking for places to visit in Western Australia then the Margaret River Region should be on top of your #Perth itinerary. The MArgaret River Region is considered to be one of the best places to visit in Australia. Located in the stunning southwest coast of Australia about 3 -4 hours drive from the City of Perth - the region is gifted with amazing sceneries, unspoilt coastlines, ancient caves and award wining wineries.

8. Pinnacles Desert
Pinnacles Desert is one of the most visited natural attraction in Western Australia  and is located in Nambung national park  about 2.5 hours north of Perth. 
How to get to Pinnacles - Need to drive from Perth or join a group tour. If you would like to join a group tour please check viator.com. You can also join stargazing at pinnacles by visiting getyourguide.com

For further information on Pinnacles National Park, please visit 
https://exploreparks.dbca.wa.gov.au/site/pinnacles-desert-lookout-and-drive9. Lancelin Sand Dunes

9. Lancelin Sand Dunes
There are many fun activities here including sandboarding, quad biking, or just stroll around and marvel at these sand dunes that were formed over 4000 years ago. 
For further information please see link below on Western Australia Tourism website. 
https://www.westernaustralia.com/au/attraction/lancelin-sand-dunes/56b267d5d5f1565045dab107
Where to hire sandboard? Please check the link below
https://www.lancelin.com.au/product/sandboard-rental-bookings/

Lancelin Beach is also a perfect spot to have a picnic - this secluded beach is one of WA's best kept holiday secrets.


10. Swan Valley
If you are looking for another day trip from Perth City and are into food and wine then the Swan Valley is for you. Swan Valley is the second oldest wine region in Australia and It is only about half an hour drive from Perth Western Australia. Discover the valley in Autumn and get to see the spectacular colour of the vineyards - from yellow. amber, gold and crimson red.

videos.title: 21 BEST Things to do in Perth, Australia | You Won't Believe What's Waiting For You (Travel Guide)
videos.description:
Discover the Allure of Perth, Western Australia!

Perth, the sunniest capital city in Australia, offers a blend of natural beauty, cultural richness, and outdoor adventures, making it a destination for everyone. Whether you’re a nature enthusiast, a foodie, or a history buff, Perth has endless experiences waiting for you.

Start your journey at Kings Park and Botanic Garden, one of the largest urban parks in the world. With sweeping views of the city skyline and Swan River, this 400-hectare oasis is perfect for walking trails, picnics, and even stargazing. For a more immersive wildlife experience, head to Yanchep National Park, where kangaroos hop around, koalas rest in eucalyptus trees, and caves invite exploration.

Looking for adventure? Rottnest Island is just a short ferry ride away. This car-free island is famous for its stunning beaches, snorkeling spots, and the adorable quokkas that call it home. For hiking enthusiasts, the Perth Hills offer trails of varying difficulty, with breathtaking views and tranquil nature.

Culture seekers will love the Art Gallery of Western Australia, home to over 17,000 works of art, including iconic pieces by Australian artists. Nearby, the Perth Cultural Centre houses museums, theaters, and galleries, creating a hub of creative energy.

Foodies will revel in Perth’s thriving culinary scene. Wander through the lively Fremantle Markets to sample fresh produce and local delicacies or enjoy specialty coffee at one of Perth’s many trendy cafes. Venture to the Swan Valley, Western Australia’s premier wine region, to indulge in world-class wines, craft beers, and gourmet cuisine.

No trip to Perth is complete without exploring the historic charm of Fremantle, a port city with a rich convict history. Visit the Fremantle Prison, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and enjoy fresh seafood at the bustling Fishing Boat Harbour.

From pristine beaches to cultural landmarks, Perth offers unforgettable experiences for every traveler. Start planning your adventure today!

videos.description: Planning to explore Perth? 🚍 Whether you're an international student, tourist, or newcomer, this video is your complete guide to using public transport in Perth, Western Australia. Learn how to catch buses, trains, and ferries, use the Transperth system, and save money with travel cards and concession fares. Perfect for anyone looking to get around Perth easily and affordably!
videos.transcript: If you're new to Perth, figuring out public transport can be tricky, but it doesn't have to be. Whether you're heading to uni, work, or just exploring the city, this guide will make it super easy. Transport is the main provider of public transport in Perth, covering buses, trains, and fairies. It's affordable, clean, and runs on a smart rider card system or paper tickets. Per's train system includes six main lines. Major stations like Perth station, Elizabeth Key, and Freemantle are key points. Trains are fast, frequent, and easy to use. Buses are great for getting around suburbs, schools, shopping centers, and to connect with train stations. In the city, CAT buses are completely free. How to get a smart rider card? Get your smart rider from transport, info centers, selected retailers, or online. It's cheaper than cash fairs, and you can register it online for balance protection. Another way to purchase them is through the train station's kiosk system. You can top up and purchase smart rider cards using your cash or card. If you don't have a smart rider card, don't worry. You can buy cash tickets from ticket machines at train stations and ferry jetties and from bus drivers. Please note that while transport buses accept cash, no change will be given, so you must pay for the exact fair. Traveling in a group? Get a family rider ticket for unlimited rides all day for up to seven people for only $10.40. Let's talk about fairs. Transport uses a zonebased system. If you're using a smart rider card, you'll save 10 to 20% compared to paying cash. Most trips cost between $280 to $520 for adults. And students get even cheaper concession fairs starting from just $110. If you're a student in Perth, you can get cheaper fairs with a student smart rider. If you're in school, ask your admin for a form or they might apply for you. If you're a uni or TA student, download the tertiary smart provider form, get it signed by your school, then submit it at a transport info center or retailer. Pay a small fee, register the card, and tap on and off to activate the discount. That's it. Easy savings on all buses, trains, and fairies. And remember, on Sundays, public transport is free. Smart rider users get unlimited travel all day. So, grab your Smart Rider, tap on and off, and travel smarter in Perth. Now, here are some quick tips for you. Always tag on and tag off. We don't want to pay for the standard fair, and we don't want to get fined. If you get caught by a transport officer, they can find you for up to $100. Now, that's a lot of money there. Also, use the transport app for lifetimes to ensure a smooth flow of your trip. You can download it to your phone via the app store. And lastly, avoid peak hours if possible. And always double check your route, especially at night since there will be a few buses and trains running. And that's it. Per public transport is clean, safe, and easy to use. Whether you're new in town or just exploring, learning the system makes life easier. Like and subscribe for more per living tips. See you guys in the next video. Bye-bye.
